sections.heading: Re-understanding how human systems interact with the living world
sections.body: Modern human systems operate at scales and speeds that increasingly exceed our systems understanding and technical, as well as ecological control. The result is accumulating risk — social, environmental, and systemic — that is perhaps properly recognized only after it becomes irreversible.
sections.highlight: We work with intent, aiming to move away from directions where damage is normalized.
sections.detail: Constraint is not the enemy of innovation or growth. Poorly understood freedom — and exploitation where we have reasonable alternatives — is.

sections.heading: Our Core Insight
sections.body: The defining challenge of industrial society is not as direct as resource scarcity. It is how resources are accessed, utilized, desired, and discarded — and the lack of rigor, accountability and oversight applied to questions of scale, precision, reversibility, and acceptable loss.
sections.detail: When interaction thresholds are crossed in complex systems, damage accumulates quietly and compounds over time. By the time consequences are visible, they are often treated as inevitable or unchangeable. Metridium exists to surface these thresholds, provide earlier alternatives to mitigate harms, and to help redesign systems so they are not crossed in the first place.
